reasoning: The cessation of Russian gas transit through Ukraine in January 2025 removed approximately 15-16 bcm/year of pipeline supply to the EU. The remaining major supply routes are TurkStream (capacity ~15.75 bcm/year) and Russian LNG deliveries (which have historically peaked around 20-22 bcm/year). Together, these routes provide a maximum theoretical run-rate of approximately 35-40 bcm/year, which equates to roughly 9-10 bcm per quarter even if running at full capacity continuously. To reach the 15 bcm threshold in a single quarter, the annualized rate would need to hit 60 bcm/year. Without the Ukraine route, Nord Stream (destroyed), or Yamal-Europe (sanctioned), reaching 15 bcm in any quarter of 2025 is a virtual impossibility barring a completely unforeseen and massive peace settlement and recommissioning of closed transit pipelines within a single quarter. Therefore, my probability is pinned to the minimum allowable threshold for this platform.
